root_text.iti8:2.1: “Mānaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.iti8:2.2: Mānañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.
root_text.iti8:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti8:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti8:2.1: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding conceit, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.iti8:2.2: By directly knowing and completely understanding conceit, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”
translation_text.iti8:3.1: “These folk are caught up in conceit,
translation_text.iti8:3.2: tied by conceit, delighting in existence.
translation_text.iti8:4.1: Those who have given up conceit,
translation_text.iti8:4.2: freed with the ending of conceit,
translation_text.iti8:4.3: vanquishers of the tie of conceit,
translation_text.iti8:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti8:4.4: sabbadukkhamupaccagun”ti → sabbaṁ ganthaṁ upaccagunti (sya-all); sabbadukkhaṁ upaccagunti (pts-vp-pli1); sabbaṁ dukkhaṁ upaccagunti (aṭṭha.)
